subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Climate Change and Future Risks
description: A 60-second lesson on Climate Change and Future Risks.

hook.text: Billion-dollar disasters in the US tripled in the last decade. The trend is accelerating.

buildup.text: Climate change intensifies hurricanes, extends wildfire seasons, and increases flood frequency.

discovery.text: Sea level rise threatens 300 million people living in coastal areas worldwide.

twist.text: Heat waves—the least dramatic disaster—kill more Americans annually than all other disasters combined.

climax.text: Adaptation planning must account for risks that didn't exist a generation ago.

punchline.text: Yesterday's 100-year flood is becoming tomorrow's Tuesday. Prepare accordingly.

quiz.question: Which disaster type kills the most Americans annually?
